publicationStmt.p: Transcribed from Bodleian Library, Lovelace Byron collection, Box 166, folios 62-64. Transcription by Lisa Gold (2022). TEI conversion for Sextant pipeline, 2026.

div.p: [horizontal line across page] The enclosed from Dr Jones today. [horizontal line across page]
This day is actually gloom personified so cloudy & rainy. My mother is worse again, & Locock has just ordered leeches again.
I am much pleased that the Hen & he seem now to agree; & both have decided to call in the aid of Dr Southey;-- not from any peculiar skill or distinction of Dr S--; but because

he is an old friend of my mother's, & a person she is very fond of, & well-calculated to assist Locock; tho' as a subordinate. My intentions are to go to Ockham tomorrow afternoon; tho' if the weather is like that of today, I certainly shall not. For it will be wretched & dreary. And I want sunshine & cheerfulness. But I am very anxious now to go

back. I long to get to my Harp. Madselle Bertucat is now gone abroad; so my practising here in Town ended on Friday. The Hen begs me to cultivate my Harp very much for the present; thinking it better calculated for me in some respects than any other study at present. And she has suggested one or two uses I may make of it for the benefit of others or at least pleasure of some
of my friends that I should well care to gratify.
Alas! Alas! the day is more & more lugubrious & dispiriting.
Direct always here. Because it loses nothing if I do go to Ockham.
Here are the two extracts I forgot yesterday.
Mrs Jameson has been most kind; & has promised whenever I go, to do all in her power to take my place with my mother.
That is a good woman, I firmly believe.
I doubt if I can go before Monday;-- on several accounts.
And if so, I had best wait for you I think; that we may go together.
But from what you say, I don't think it is very certain that you can come on Monday even.
And my arrangements are as you perceive as yet absolutely uncertain.
I think it serves me right to have such a dose of London now.
